step_by_step_thinking: Given that the variable being studied is continuous (birth weights measured in grams) and there is only one categorical predictor (maternal intake of iron supplements [yes/no]), we should use a **t-test** to compare means between two groups. Since the sample sizes are equal (as implied by having collected data from exactly 100 pregnant women where binary exposure was noted without further subgrouping), an unpaired/t-independent t-test would appropriately assess whether there's a statistically significant difference in mean birth weights between those taking iron supplements versus those who do not.
